Assembly: This project presents the diploid genome assembly of the green sea urchin, Strongylocentrotus droebachiensis, from a sample provided by Kim Prabel. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The eeStrDroe3 assembly is based on 55x PacBio HiFi and 51x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size.

Assembly: The assembly ilBucCida1.hap1.1 is based on 83x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). The order and orientation of contigs is uncertain in the following regions: chromosome 24, 4.4 Mb to 5.8 Mb; chromosome 27, 9.1 Mb to 10 Mb.

Assembly: The assembly of Myripristis jacobus (fMyrJac1) is based on 33X ONT data and 210X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. No contigs were found to be contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral). Additionally, 152 regions totaling 13.263 Mb (with the largest being 0.392 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. During manual curation, 6 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 1.21Mb (with the largest being 0.49Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly of Aidablennius sphynx (fAidSph1) is based on 66X PacBio data and 170X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr).The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 95 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 5.5 Mb (with the largest being 2.8Mb). Additionally, 1,299 regions totaling 33.5 Mb (with the largest being 0.218 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using oatk. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 13 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 1.7 Gb (with the largest being 0.49 Mb). 1718 scaffolds assigned to Platyhelminthes phylum were removed, totaling 60Mb. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: Assembly and annotation of a wild type Brassica oleracea individual genome (BOS01). Nanopore reads were assembled using NextDenovo. The resulting contigs were first polished with Medaka, using default parameters and Nanopore long reads, and then further polished with two rounds of Hapo-G, also using default parameters and Illumina short reads. The polished assemblies were scaffolded using Ragtag with the Brassica oleracea cv. Korso genome as the reference.

Assembly: This project provides the diploid genome assembly bTurPil1.1 of the fieldfare, Turdus pilaris. The sample was provided by Jan T. Lifjeld, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bTurPil1.1 is based on 22x PacBio HiFi data and 48x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Assembly: This project has been funded by the Federation Nationale des Chasseurs (FR). The assembly of Turdus iliacus (bTurIli2.1) is based on 51X ONT data and 35X Dovetail Hi-C data. The assembly process included the following steps: initial nanopore assembly generation with Nextdenovo, polishing of the consensus using Medaka (with long-reads) and Hapo-G (with short-reads), removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. No contigs were found to be contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral). Additionally, 1,524 regions totaling 492 Mb (with the largest being 3.8 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 14 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 4.20 Mb (with the largest being 898 Kb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. Sexual chromosomes Z and W were identified using comparative genomics and organization of microchromosomes was curated using MicroFinder.

The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Nextdenovo, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 1 contig was identified as contaminant (bacterial) totaling 37 kb (with the largest being 37 kb). Additionally, 444 regions totaling 177 Mb (with the largest being 6 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 1 haplotypic region was removed, totaling 573 kb. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. Sexual chromosomes Z and W were identified using comparative genomics and organization of microchromosomes was curated using MicroFinder.

Assembly: The assembly dmBerAqui1.hap1.1 is based on 49x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The following regions of this assembly consist of scaffolds in an uncertain order and orientation: Chromosome 3 ~35.5-43.9 Mbp, Chromosome 6 ~41.5-56.8 Mbp, Chromosome 8 ~31.6-39 Mbp, Chromosome 12 ~30.9-35.3 Mbp and Chromosome 14 ~16.4-23.7 Mbp. The following regions represent heterozygous inversions: Chromosome 1 ~23.1-34.5 Mbp (multiple), Chromosome 7 ~25-27 Mbp (single), Chromosome 9 ~34.3-37.8 Mbp (double) and Chromosome 11 ~27.3-29.7 (single) and ~44.3-47.1 Mbp (double).

Assembly: Primary assembly. Maternal haplotig of Trio-binned genome of a male wild-type naked mole-rat. Reads in the initial assebmly were a combination of the maternal- and unassigned- reads. Assembly was completed by a combination of the vertebrate genome project and DNA-zoo projects. Mitochondrial genomes were assembled with the mitoVGP pipeline. Y-chromosome contigs were generated with a custom pipeline that levaraged male and female WGS to find strongly male-biased contigs. Male-biased contigs were re-assembled with public male NMR Hi-C data. This assembly was then upraded by employing in silico chromosome painting of the draft scaffolds in the assembly against the American Porcupine (EreDor1) assembly and against the NMR FISH-Karyoptype published in Romanenko et al., 2023 (PMID: 380307020). This upgrade corrected intrachromosomal misassemblies, placed small contigs, and placed centromeres. Ambiguous syntenic alignments were resolved by inspecting our Hi-C-seq data at the end points of those regions. Chromosome names were identified in collaboration with GeneCodehc.

Assembly: The assembly neRomNiel1.hap1.1 is based on 336x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. While the sequenced sample (SAMEA112301908) has no large chromosomal rearrangements on chromosome 1 between the haplotypes, an alternate sample (SAMEA118348788), which was used for HiC, shows an inversion at 37.305-38.548.

Assembly: The assembly of Phycis phycis (fPhyPhy1) is based on 46X ONT data and 205X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. No contigs were found to be contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral). Additionally, 71 regions totaling 14.251 Mb (with the largest being 5.69 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK and PacBio data initially generated for this assembly. During manual curation, one haplotypic region totaling 1.05 Mb was removed. Chromosomes fPhyPhy1_24 and fPhyPhy1_25 appear to be sex chromosomes, as they show approximately half the coverage observed for other chromosomes. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.
